exampleSentences: tôi gọi một ly cà phê.

translation.en: I really like to eat pho.

notes:
tôi = “I” (the speaker). It’s neutral and polite, safe to use in most situations, especially with people you don’t know well.

It works as a subject pronoun before verbs or adjectives.  

Compared to other “I” words: mình is softer/more intimate, tao is rough or very informal. If you’re unsure, tôi is the safe choice.
sentenceBreakdowns: tôi (I) + gọi (order) + một (a) + ly (cup) + cà phê (coffee)
notes_md:

It works as a subject pronoun before verbs or adjectives.  

Compared to other “I” words: **mình** is softer/more intimate, **tao** is rough or very informal. If you’re unsure, **tôi** is the safe choice.

translation.en: I really like to eat pho.

definitions.es: Yo (pronombre de primera persona del singular)
definitions.vi: Tôi (đại từ nhân xưng ngôi thứ nhất số ít)
